diagrams.title: Precedence Graph Example - Conflict Serializable
diagrams.description: Shows a precedence graph with no cycle, indicating conflict serializable schedule
diagrams.purpose: Learn to construct and interpret precedence graphs for serializability testing

diagrams.title: Precedence Graph Example - Not Serializable
diagrams.description: Shows a precedence graph with cycle, indicating non-serializable schedule
elements: Explanation: conflicting operations create circular dependency
diagrams.purpose: Understand how cycles in precedence graph indicate non-serializability

diagrams.title: Schedule Properties Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Pyramid diagram showing hierarchy from most restrictive to least restrictive schedule types
diagrams.purpose: Visualize relationship between different schedule properties

diagrams.title: Conflict Operations Matrix
diagrams.description: 2x2 matrix showing which operation pairs create conflicts
diagrams.purpose: Quick reference for identifying conflicting operations

diagrams.title: View Serializability Conditions
diagrams.description: Three conditions required for view equivalence between schedules
elements: Condition 1: Initial Read - same transaction reads each data item first
Condition 2: Read-From - same transaction each read depends on
Condition 3: Final Write - same transaction performs final write on each item
diagrams.purpose: Understand criteria for view equivalence and view serializability testing
